
\# Cycle Design

> Cycle Design is the design system for Fluencypass. It uses **shadcn/ui** as the component base, customized with Cycle Design foundations (colors, typography, spacing, shadows, radius). Built with React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, and Radix UI primitives. Supports light and dark themes via `[data-theme]` attribute. The Figma remains the source of truth for visual values.

## Getting Started

- [Overview](ai/overview.md): Architecture, token categories, brand palettes, and mandatory rules for using Cycle Design.
- [Installation](ai/installation.md): How to install the package, import tokens, load fonts, and file structure.
- [Dark Mode](ai/dark-mode.md): Theme switching via data-theme attribute, prefers-color-scheme support, and implementation guide.

## Tokens

- [Colors](ai/tokens/colors.md): Complete color system with 11 primitive palettes and functional tokens for text, border, foreground, and background. Includes semantic (critical, warning, positive) and 5 brand palettes.
- [Typography](ai/tokens/typography.md): Font primitives (families, sizes, weights, line-heights) and 38 composition classes (body, subtitle, headline, display, button).
- [Spacing](ai/tokens/spacing.md): 16 gap tokens (2px to 200px) and 7 inset/padding tokens (4px to 40px).
- [Shadows](ai/tokens/shadows.md): 7 elevation levels from subtle (xs) to ultra emphasis (3xl), using multi-layer box shadows.
- [Borders & Radius](ai/tokens/borders-radius.md): 5 border width tokens and 9 radius tokens (none to circular).
- [Opacity](ai/tokens/opacity.md): 7 opacity levels from transparent (0) to opaque (1).
- [Gradients](ai/tokens/gradients.md): 13 gray gradients and 5 brand gradients with light/dark mode support.
- [Grid](ai/tokens/grid.md): Mobile-first responsive grid with 5 breakpoints (320px to 1584px), CSS Grid classes, and panel layout support.
- [Motion](ai/tokens/motion.md): Duration tokens (instant to slower), easing curves (default, in, out, linear), and composition shortcuts for transitions.
- [Z-Index](ai/tokens/z-index.md): Stacking scale for overlay components (dropdown, sticky, overlay, modal, popover, toast, tooltip).

## Guidelines

- [Design Decisions](ai/decisions.md): Architecture decisions, Storybook rationale, AI integration strategy, protections against mistakes, and update flow.
- [Improvement Plan](ai/improvement-plan.md): Quality improvement roadmap (motion tokens, reduced-motion, z-index, asChild, data attributes, compound components, cn utility, Figma mapping). Complementary to the action plan.
- [Analysis vs shadcn/ui](ai/analysis-vs-shadcn.md): Comparative quality analysis against shadcn/ui — strengths, gaps, and recommendations.
- [Compound Components](ai/patterns/compound-components.md): Guide for when and how to use compound components pattern vs monolithic props.
- [Figma Mapping](ai/figma-mapping.md): Complete Figma → Code translation reference for all tokens, typography, colors, spacing, and components.
- [Testing Checklist](ai/testing-checklist.md): Mandatory 4-layer testing checklist for components (rendering, interaction, accessibility, data attributes) and token contract tests.

## shadcn/ui Integration

- [shadcn Architecture](ai/shadcn-architecture.md): Architectural decision to use shadcn/ui as component base. Layer diagram, variable mapping, setup steps, and rules for AI.
- [shadcn Theme Mapping](ai/shadcn-theme-mapping.md): Complete globals.css with all shadcn CSS variables mapped to Cycle Design values (light/dark). Ready to copy-paste.
- [shadcn Migration](ai/shadcn-migration.md): Migration guide from old hand-built components to shadcn/ui. Stack comparison, component mapping, and setup steps.
- [Figma Setup for shadcn](ai/figma-shadcn-guide.md): Step-by-step guide for designers to configure the Figma library aligned with shadcn/ui variables. Includes every component, every variable, every decision.

## Documentation

- [Installation Guide](docs/INSTALLATION.md): Complete setup guide for new projects using Cycle Design with shadcn/ui. Covers Next.js, Vite, fonts, dark mode, product palettes, and all available components.

## MCP Server

- [MCP Server](ai/mcp.md): Model Context Protocol server for AI integrations. Allows AI assistants to browse tokens, search documentation, and look up specific CSS variables using natural language. Works with Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code, and more.
